mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive days) during periods when CPIAUCSL shows sustained year-over-year growth above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat (average return < 0.01%) in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is decelerating while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it indicates a significant headwind for consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where RSAFS year-over-year growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, a strong rebound in housing starts indicates renewed economic activity and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations improve over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, a month-over-month increase in HOUST exceeding 10% does not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 70% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and supply chain activity, indicating a healthy economic environment that supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative (average return < -0.05%) during periods of strong INDPRO year-over-year growth (e.g., > 3%).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a critical threshold, it signifies a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the cost of capital and reduces economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive days) when the FEDFUNDS rate is consistently above 4.5% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust payroll employment growth and strong GDP expansion indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong foundation for corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) when both PAYEMS shows >0.1% month-over-month growth and GDPC1 shows >2% annualized quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP, especially after a prolonged expansion, signals an impending or ongoing recession, which severely impacts corporate earnings and leads to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate turns negative quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive or flat (average return < 0.01%) following a confirmed quarter-over-quarter decline in GDPC1 during periods identified as late-cycle or recession onset.
